## FHIR Queries: Crohn's Disease Patients with Multiple Encounters & IBD Medications

> **Note on "multiple encounters":** FHIR has no native `count > N` filter. Retrieve all encounter records and aggregate/filter by patient on the client side to identify those with ≥ 2 encounters.

---

### 🔷 Step 1 — Crohn's Disease Conditions

**SNOMED CT**
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|34000006&_include=Condition:patient
```

**ICD-10-CM (K50.x — hierarchical match)**
```
Condition?code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K50&_include=Condition:patient
```

---

### 🔷 Step 2 — Encounters with Crohn's Diagnosis
*(Used to identify patients with multiple encounters carrying this diagnosis)*

**SNOMED CT**
```
Encounter?reason-code=http://snomed.info/sct|34000006&_include=Encounter:patient&_include=Encounter:diagnosis
```

**ICD-10-CM (hierarchical match)**
```
Encounter?reason-code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K50&_include=Encounter:patient&_include=Encounter:diagnosis
```

---

### 🔷 Step 3 — IBD Medication Prescriptions

**Anti-TNF Biologics** *(infliximab, adalimumab, certolizumab pegol)*
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|185897,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|327361,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|559762&status=active,completed&_include=MedicationRequest:patient&_include=MedicationRequest:medication
```

**Anti-Integrin Biologics** *(vedolizumab, natalizumab)*
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1535228,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|354770&status=active,completed&_include=MedicationRequest:patient&_include=MedicationRequest:medication
```

**Anti-IL-12/23 & Anti-IL-23 Biologics** *(ustekinumab, risankizumab)*
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1162004,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|2200644&status=active,completed&_include=MedicationRequest:patient&_include=MedicationRequest:medication
```

**Immunomodulators** *(azathioprine, mercaptopurine, methotrexate)*
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1356,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|38268,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|8814&status=active,completed&_include=MedicationRequest:patient&_include=MedicationRequest:medication
```

**Aminosalicylates & Targeted Steroids** *(mesalamine, budesonide)*
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|41493,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|19831&status=active,completed&_include=MedicationRequest:patient&_include=MedicationRequest:medication
```

**JAK Inhibitors** *(upadacitinib, tofacitinib)*
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|2049106,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1151415&status=active,completed&_include=MedicationRequest:patient&_include=MedicationRequest:medication
```

---

### 🔷 Step 4 — Combined Patient Query (Crohn's + IBD Meds via `_has`)
*(Single query to find patients satisfying both conditions simultaneously)*

```
Patient?_has:Condition:patient:code=http://snomed.info/sct|34000006&_has:MedicationRequest:patient:code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|185897,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|327361,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|559762,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1535228,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|354770,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1162004,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|2200644,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1356,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|38268,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|8814,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|41493,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|19831,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|2049106,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1151415&_revinclude=Condition:patient&_revinclude=Encounter:patient&_revinclude=MedicationRequest:patient
```

---

### 1️⃣ Crohn's Disease Conditions (SNOMED CT + ICD-10-CM)
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|34000006,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K50.0,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K50.1,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K50.8,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K50.9&clinical-status=active,recurrence,relapse&_include=Condition:subject

```

1-expert.prompt_text: Find all patients diagnosed with Crohn's disease who have had multiple encounters with the diagnosis and have been prescribed medications used to treat inflammatory bowel disease.

2-naive.raw_response:
All data gathered. The combined `_has` query returns 1,117 patients with both a Crohn's Condition and an IBD MedicationRequest. Now I have everything needed to assemble the final, comprehensive queries.

**Architecture of the three-query approach:**
- **Query 1 (Conditions):** All Crohn's subtypes (39 SNOMED + 28 ICD-10-CM + 4 ICD-9-CM codes) filtered to `encounter-diagnosis` category → *client groups records by `patient` and keeps only patients with ≥2 records (= multiple encounters with the diagnosis)*
- **Query 2 (Medications):** All IBD drug classes via RxNorm — aminosalicylates (mesalamine, sulfasalazine), biologics/TNF-inhibitors (infliximab, adalimumab, certolizumab pegol, golimumab), integrin antagonists (vedolizumab, natalizumab), IL-12/23 inhibitors (ustekinumab), immunomodulators (azathioprine, mercaptopurine, methotrexate), gut-selective corticosteroids (budesonide)
- **Query 3 (Patient intersection):** `_has` reverse-chain enforcing BOTH Crohn's Condition AND IBD MedicationRequest at the Patient level simultaneously (the 1,117-patient core intersection)

2-naive.prompt_text: Find all patients diagnosed with Crohn's disease who have had multiple encounters with the diagnosis and have been prescribed medications used to treat inflammatory bowel disease.

2-broad.raw_response:
All data validated:
- **2,080 Crohn's encounter-diagnosis Conditions** (multi-encounter capable)
- **2,328 IBD medication records** 
- **1,755 patients with BOTH** Crohn's diagnosis AND IBD medications (`_has` query confirms server supports this)

Here's my full reasoning summary before the final answer:

---

### Cohort Decomposition
| Requirement | Resource | Strategy |
|---|---|---|
| Crohn's diagnosis | `Condition` | SNOMED (39 codes, VSAC MITRE OID 2.16.840.1.113762.1.4.1032.346) + ICD-10-CM (28 codes K50.xx, VSAC OID 2.16.840.1.113762.1.4.1032.347) |
| **Multiple encounters** | `Condition` + `category=encounter-diagnosis` | Server tags each encounter-linked dx as `encounter-diagnosis`; runner groups by `patient` and keeps those with **≥2** records |
| IBD medications | `MedicationRequest` | RxNorm ingredients: mesalamine (52582), infliximab (191831), adalimumab (327361), azathioprine (1256), sulfasalazine (9524), methotrexate (6851), budesonide (19831) — all confirmed on server |

### Logic
- **Query 1** → Groups by `patient`, keeps those with **≥2** encounter-diagnosis Condition records (= multiple Crohn's encounters)
- **Query 2** → All IBD MedicationRequests
- **Final cohort** = Patient IDs in both sets (intersection)

2-broad.prompt_text: Find all patients diagnosed with Crohn's disease who have had multiple encounters with the diagnosis and have been prescribed medications used to treat inflammatory bowel disease.

2-expert.raw_response:
The combined `_has` query returns **843 patients** with BOTH a Crohn's diagnosis and an IBD medication. The Condition query returns **2080** encounter-level diagnosis records across those patients — the runner will group by patient and filter for ≥2 records (= multiple encounters). I have all I need to build the final queries.

Here's a summary of what was discovered before forming the final answer:

| Track | Finding |
|---|---|
| **Server** | FHIR R4 (Azure), RxNorm-only for meds, SNOMED+ICD-10-CM+ICD-9-CM for conditions |
| **Crohn's Dx codes** | Full VSAC MITRE set: 39 SNOMED + 28 ICD-10-CM + 4 ICD-9-CM codes = **2,080 encounter-level records** |
| **IBD medications on server** | Mesalamine (RxNorm `52582`), Infliximab (`191831`), Adalimumab (`327361`) |
| **No Encounter resources** | No `Encounter` resources exist — multiple encounters inferred from ≥2 `Condition` records per patient with `category=encounter-diagnosis` |
| **AND intersection** | `Patient?_has:Condition+_has:MedicationRequest` returns **843 patients** |

3-naive.prompt_text: Find all patients diagnosed with Crohn's disease who have had multiple encounters with the diagnosis and have been prescribed medications used to treat inflammatory bowel disease.

3-broad.raw_response:
1,559 validated patients confirmed — the cross-resource AND query works perfectly. The server has no Encounter resources; instead Conditions carry an `encounter-diagnosis` category, meaning each distinct Condition record = one documented encounter. Multiple Conditions per patient = multiple encounters.

Here is my full reasoning before the final answer:

---

### Playbooks Applied
| Playbook | Rationale |
|---|---|
| **11 — Cross-resource AND** | Requires Crohn's Condition **AND** IBD MedicationRequest for the same patient (`_has` chaining) |
| **12 — Validated Case** | "Multiple encounters + prescribed medications" = strict AND across resources, not a loose union |
| **8 — Multi-system code list** | Server uses SNOMED + ICD-10-CM + ICD-9-CM for Condition; all included |
| **1 — Subtypes** | Crohn's has small intestine, large intestine, combined, remission, exacerbation variants — umbrella SNOMED plus all K50.x ICD-10 subtypes |

### Code Evidence
| Source | Coverage |
|---|---|
| **SNOMED** | `34000006` (parent), `56689002` (SI), `7620006` (LB), `196977009` (terminal ileum), `71833008` (SI+LI), `50440006` (colon), `38106008` (ileum), `426549001` (remission), `414153008/414154002` (exacerbation) |
| **ICD-10-CM** | Full K50.0x–K50.9x spectrum (MITRE VSAC 2.16.840.1.113762.1.4.1032.347) |
| **ICD-9-CM** | 555.0, 555.1, 555.9 (observed on server) |
| **RxNorm meds (ingredient-level, confirmed on server)** | adalimumab `327361`, infliximab `191831`, azathioprine `1256`, ustekinumab `847083`, sulfasalazine `9524` |

### Counts
| Query | Count |
|---|---|
| Patients with any Crohn's dx | 624 |
| MedicationRequests for IBD biologics/immunomods | 2,044 |
| **Validated case: Crohn's dx AND IBD medication** | **1,559** |

### "Multiple encounters" note
No Encounter resources exist on this server. Conditions carry `category=encounter-diagnosis`, so each Condition record represents one documented clinical encounter. **Query 2** retrieves all raw Crohn's encounter-diagnosis records; the downstream application layer counts records per patient and filters to ≥ 2 to enforce the multiple-encounter criterion.

---

## FHIR Queries: Crohn's Disease + Biologic Medication (via `_has` Reverse Chaining)

---

### Query Logic Architecture

```
Patient  ◄──_has:Condition:patient:code──  Condition (Crohn's dx)
         ◄──_has:MedicationRequest:patient:code──  MedicationRequest (biologic Rx)

Multiple _has parameters = AND at Patient level
Comma-separated codes within each _has = OR (any matching code qualifies)
```

---

### Query 1 — Primary: Patient-Level AND Gate *(returns matched Patient resources)*

```

```

### Code Reference Table

| Domain | Code System | Code | Display |
|--------|------------|------|---------|
| **Crohn's Disease** | SNOMED CT | `34000006` | Crohn's disease |
| | ICD-10-CM | `K50` | Crohn's disease (parent) |
| | ICD-10-CM | `K50.0` | Crohn's disease of small intestine |
| | ICD-10-CM | `K50.1` | Crohn's disease of large intestine |
| | ICD-10-CM | `K50.8` | Other Crohn's disease |
| | ICD-10-CM | `K50.9` | Crohn's disease, unspecified |
| **Anti-TNF Biologics** | RxNorm | `241493` | Infliximab (Remicade) |
| | RxNorm | `327361` | Adalimumab (Humira) |
| | RxNorm | `559692` | Certolizumab pegol (Cimzia) |
| **Other IBD Biologics** | RxNorm | `1535149` | Vedolizumab (Entyvio) — gut-selective anti-integrin |
| | RxNorm | `1110983` | Ustekinumab (Stelara) — anti-IL-12/23 |

---

### Design Notes

| Concern | Resolution |
|---------|------------|
| **Anti-TNF disambiguation** | The first `_has` block requires a confirmed Crohn's dx code — patients with only RA who happen to be on adalimumab will be excluded |
| **`code` vs `medication` parameter** | `code` targets `medicationCodeableConcept` (inline code); use `medication` if your server stores a `Medication` reference instead |
| **ICD-10 subsumption** | If your server supports it, replace explicit K50.x list with `code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm\|K50` |
| **`_revinclude` scope** | Without additional filters, `_revinclude=Condition:patient` returns *all* conditions; post-filter client-side by code |
| **Comma = OR** | Within a single `_has` parameter, comma-separated values are `OR`; separate `_has` parameters are `AND` |

## FHIR Query — Crohn's Disease Patients on Biologics (Intersection via `_has`)

```
Patient?_has:Condition:patient:code=http://snomed.info/sct|34000006,http://snomed.info/sct|7620006,http://snomed.info/sct|196977009,http://snomed.info/sct|1197732001,http://snomed.info/sct|1197733006&_has:MedicationRequest:patient:code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|191831,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|327361,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|847083
```

---

### Query Breakdown

| `_has` Block | Resource | Link Param | Search Param | Values |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **First** | `Condition` | `patient` | `code` | SNOMED Crohn's codes (OR logic via `,`) |
| **Second** | `MedicationRequest` | `patient` | `code` | RxNorm biologic codes (OR logic via `,`) |

---

### Code Annotations

**Crohn's SNOMED Codes (Condition)**

| Code | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `34000006` | Crohn's disease (general) |
| `7620006` | Crohn's disease of small intestine |
| `196977009` | Crohn's disease of large intestine |
| `1197732001` | Crohn's disease of upper GI tract |
| `1197733006` | Crohn's disease of perianal region |

**Biologic RxNorm Codes (MedicationRequest)**

| Code | Drug |
|---|---|
| `191831` | Infliximab |
| `327361` | Adalimumab |
| `847083` | Ustekinumab |

---

### Intersection Logic

```
Patient result set =
  { patients with ANY Crohn's Condition }
    ∩
  { patients with ANY Crohn's biologic MedicationRequest }
```

> ⚠️ **Note:** The comma-separated codes within each `_has` block are **OR** (any matching code satisfies that block). The two separate `_has` parameters are **AND** (both blocks must be satisfied by the *same* patient). This is the key distinction that makes `_has` chaining an intersection query rather than a union.

Use FHIR `_has` parameter on the Patient resource: `Patient?_has:Condition:patient:code=...&_has:MedicationRequest:patient:code=...`. The double `_has` ensures both conditions are met for the same patient (intersection, not union).

✅ Returns 814 Patient resources — confirmed correct. The query returns only `Patient` resource type, and the count is stable whether using partial or full code enumeration, meaning coverage is complete.

Here is a full breakdown of every design decision made:

---

## 🔬 Discovery & Design Rationale

### 1. Server Profile (FHIR 4.0.1, Azure — no IGs)
- No US Core loaded → no `Condition.category=problem-list-item` required
- `:below` (SNOMED subsumption) is **not supported** → must enumerate all subtypes explicitly
- MedicationRequest uses **RxNorm ingredient-level codes** (no product-level or NDC)

### 2. Crohn's Disease Code Family (3 code systems, VSAC MITRE OIDs)

| Source | Codes Used | Count |
|---|---|---|
| **SNOMED CT** | 25 site-specific subtypes (large bowel, small intestine, ileum, colon, rectum, duodenum, jejunum, esophagus, stomach, anastomosis, appendix, etc.) + parent `34000006` | 25 |
| **ICD-10-CM** | Full K50.* family: K50.00–K50.919 (small intestine, large intestine, both, unspecified × complication variants) | 28 |
| **ICD-9-CM** | 555.0, 555.1, 555.2, 555.9 (regional enteritis — legacy records) | 4 |

> **Why subsumption matters but `:below` failed:** SNOMED confirmed `34000006` subsumes `7620006`, `56689002`, etc. — but the Azure FHIR server returns HTTP 403 for `:below`. Explicit enumeration is required.

### 3. Anti-TNF Biologics (RxNorm ingredient level)
- `191831` — **infliximab** (reference biologic; Remicade) — 555 prescriptions on server
- `327361` — **adalimumab** (reference biologic; Humira) — 530 prescriptions on server
- ⚠️ Both drugs are also used in RA, psoriasis, ankylosing spondylitis — the Crohn's Condition `_has` arm is the **required disambiguator**

### 4. `_has` Reverse Chain — AND semantics at the Patient level
`_has:Condition:patient:code=<crohns>` AND `_has:MedicationRequest:patient:code=<biologics>` — both arms must be satisfied by the **same patient**, which is exactly the intersection logic required.

1-naive.prompt_text: Find biologic patients without Crohn's.

1-broad.raw_response:
## Two-Query Subtraction Strategy

**Logic:** `Result = SET_A (biologic patients) − SET_B (Crohn's patients)`

---

### SET A — All Patients Prescribed These Biologics

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|354770,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|327360,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1167603&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:patient&_include=MedicationRequest:requester
```

---

### SET B — Patients WITH Crohn's Disease (Exclusion List)

**Via SNOMED CT (with subsumption — catches all subtypes):**
```
Condition?code:below=http://snomed.info/sct|34000006&clinical-status=active,recurrence,relapse&_elements=subject
```

**Via ICD-10-CM (K50.x hierarchy):**
```
Condition?code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K50&_elements=subject
```

---

### Code Reference

| Drug | RxNorm CUI | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Infliximab (Remicade) | `354770` | Anti-TNF; RA, PsA, IBD |
| Adalimumab (Humira) | `327360` | Anti-TNF; RA, PsA, IBD |
| Ustekinumab (Stelara) | `1167603` | IL-12/23 inhibitor; CD, PsA |
| Crohn's disease (SNOMED) | `34000006` | Use `:below` for subtypes |
| Crohn's disease (ICD-10) | `K50` | Use `:below` for K50.0x–K50.9x |

---

### Application-Layer Logic

```
biologic_patients  = extract patient IDs from SET_A bundle
crohns_patients    = extract subject IDs from SET_B (2a ∪ 2b)

target_population  = biologic_patients - crohns_patients
# → Expected: RA, PsA, ankylosing spondylitis, plaque psoriasis patients
```

> **Notes:**
> - SET B intentionally omits `clinical-status` on the ICD-10 query to catch historical/resolved diagnoses (conservative exclusion)
> - SNOMED `:below` and ICD-10 `:below` require server-side subsumption support (FHIR R4+); if unsupported, enumerate leaf codes (K50.00–K50.919)
> - Add `&_count=1000&_page=...` for pagination on large datasets

The expected population is mostly rheumatoid arthritis patients (anti-TNFs are first-line for RA). Use a two-query subtraction approach since FHIR does not support negative chains.

1-expert.raw_response:
## FHIR Queries for Set Difference

### Query 1 — Biologic MedicationRequests (Set A)

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|191831,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|327361,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|847083&status=active,on-hold&_elements=subject,id,status,medicationCodeableConcept&_count=1000
```

### Query 2 — Crohn's Conditions (Set B / exclusion set)

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|34000006,http://snomed.info/sct|7620006,http://snomed.info/sct|196977009,http://snomed.info/sct|1197732001,http://snomed.info/sct|1197733006&clinical-status=active,recurrence,relapse&_elements=subject,id,clinicalStatus,code&_count=1000
```

---

## Set Difference Logic (Client-Side)

FHIR R4 has no native `NOT EXISTS` / anti-join, so the subtraction is performed in application code:

```
Set A  →  extract patient IDs from MedicationRequest.subject.reference
Set B  →  extract patient IDs from Condition.subject.reference
Result →  Set A  \  Set B   (patients in A not present in B)
```

---

## ⚠️ Critical Caveats

| Concern | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Pagination** | Both queries may return `>1000` resources; follow all `Bundle.link[rel=next]` pages before computing the difference |
| **MedicationRequest status** | `active,on-hold` used above — include `completed` if historical biologic exposure matters for your cohort |
| **Condition clinical-status** | `active,recurrence,relapse` excludes *resolved* Crohn's; change to omit `clinical-status` filter if resolved history should still exclude a patient |
| **ICD-10 gap** | Crohn's Set B uses SNOMED only — many EHRs store `http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K50.0`, `K50.1`, `K50.8`, `K50.9`; add a parallel Condition query or use `$translate` to avoid under-exclusion |
| **Duplicate patients** | A single patient may appear in multiple resources; deduplicate on `Patient/{id}` before differencing |
| **Indication not linked** | MedicationRequest lacks a required `reasonReference`/`reasonCode` in most real data, so the "RA-on-biologics" interpretation is inferential only |

### Supplemental ICD-10 Crohn's exclusion query (recommended)

```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K50.0,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K50.1,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K50.8,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K50.9,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K50.10,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K50.11,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K50.80,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K50.90&clinical-status=active,recurrence,relapse&_elements=subject,id&_count=1000
```

Union the patient IDs from both Condition queries into a single Set B before computing `A \ B`.

From clinical experience, Crohn's patients are documented in different ways: most have a formal Crohn's diagnosis (often location-specific like terminal ileum or colon), most are on IBD medications (mesalamine, biologics like infliximab/adalimumab/ustekinumab, or immunomodulators like azathioprine), and many have elevated fecal calprotectin during disease flares.

Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:
1. Patients with any Crohn's disease diagnosis (parent Crohn's, large bowel, terminal ileum, colon and rectum, or jejunum and ileum)
2. Patients prescribed IBD medications (mesalamine, infliximab, adalimumab, ustekinumab, or azathioprine)
3. Patients with elevated fecal calprotectin (>250 mcg/g)

For each query, return the matching resources. I want the union of all patients found.

1-broad.raw_response:
## 📋 Implementation Notes *(continued)*

| Topic | Guidance |
|---|---|
| **Ustekinumab code** | Verify RxNorm `847746` against your local formulary — some systems store biosimilars under separate RXCUIs (e.g., ustekinumab-aauz, ustekinumab-auub); search by brand name "Stelara" as a cross-check |
| **Missing biologics** | Consider adding vedolizumab (`1440102`), certolizumab pegol (`878685`), and risankizumab (`2362725`) to the medication queries — all FDA-approved for Crohn's and may represent patients in your population |
| **6-Mercaptopurine** | Azathioprine is a prodrug of 6-MP; add RxNorm `35469` (mercaptopurine) to capture patients prescribed 6-MP directly |
| **Observation units** | Some labs report calprotectin as `mg/kg` (numerically equivalent to `ug/g`) — if your lab uses `mg/kg`, substitute `|http://unitsofmeasure.org|mg/kg` in the Observation query |
| **Date scoping** | To focus on recently active patients, add `&recorded-date=ge2024-01-01` to Condition queries or `&date=ge2024-01-01` to Observation queries |
| **Verification status** | Optionally add `&verification-status=confirmed` to Condition queries to exclude suspected or provisional diagnoses |
| **Infusion records** | Infliximab and vedolizumab are often administered in infusion centers — cross-reference `Procedure` resources with SNOMED `410942007` (drug administration) or CPT codes for biologic infusions |

---

Condition?code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K50&clinical-status=active&_include=Condition:patient

# 1B: SNOMED CT hierarchical (preferred)

# 1C-i: ICD-10 explicit — Small intestine / terminal ileum (K50.0x)

# ── GROUP 2: MEDICATIONS ─────────────────────────────────────────────

### 📌 Key Implementation Notes for your QI Team

1. **Code multiplicity is real**: Conditions carry SNOMED + ICD-10-CM + ICD-9-CM simultaneously. Any single-system query captures the same 2,080 records; the multi-system query is defensive for edge cases.
2. **Medication codes must be ingredient-level**: Product-level RxNorm codes (e.g., "azathioprine Injectable Product" `1155000`) returned **zero** results. The server stores `1256` (azathioprine ingredient) only.
3. **Ustekinumab & adalimumab are well-represented**: 414 and 530 prescriptions respectively — these are your biologic adherence cohort.
4. **Fecal calprotectin threshold**: `gt250` implements the clinically accepted >250 mcg/g threshold for active IBD. The 372 elevated results are your disease flare monitoring sub-cohort.
5. **Patient-level union**: Union the patient references (`subject`) across all three result sets to get your full unduplicated Crohn's population.

2-broad.prompt_text:
I need to identify my complete Crohn's disease patient population for a quality improvement initiative on biologic therapy adherence and disease activity monitoring. From clinical experience, Crohn's patients are documented in different ways: most have a formal Crohn's diagnosis (often location-specific like terminal ileum or colon), most are on IBD medications (mesalamine, biologics like infliximab/adalimumab/ustekinumab, or immunomodulators like azathioprine), and many have elevated fecal calprotectin during disease flares.

Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:
1. Patients with any Crohn's disease diagnosis (parent Crohn's, large bowel, terminal ileum, colon and rectum, or jejunum and ileum)
2. Patients prescribed IBD medications (mesalamine, infliximab, adalimumab, ustekinumab, or azathioprine)
3. Patients with elevated fecal calprotectin (>250 mcg/g)

For each query, return the matching resources. I want the union of all patients found.

### 💡 Implementation Notes for Your QI Initiative
1. **Union, not intersection** — patients qualify if they appear in ANY of the three queries. A patient on infliximab without a formal diagnosis code is still a Crohn's patient worth monitoring.
2. **Mesalamine enrichment** — the 698 patients on mesalamine uniquely (not captured by dx codes) are likely mild/early-disease or patients with IBD-overlap whose diagnosis coding is incomplete; they're high-value targets for your adherence initiative.
3. **Fecal calprotectin cohort** — the 372 patients with elevated calprotectin may include those in active flare not yet on biologics — a key escalation-monitoring group for your initiative.
4. **Remission codes included** — SNOMED `426549001` (Crohn's in remission) is deliberately included because these patients still require biologic maintenance monitoring.

## 📋 Union Strategy Notes

| Query | Signal Type | Clinical Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Q1 – Diagnoses | Formal ICD/SNOMED coding | Most reliable for established Crohn's cases; coded at encounter, includes remission codes |
| Q2 – Medications | IBD-specific drug prescriptions | Catches patients managed by medication alone, or whose dx code is generic (e.g. K50.90) |
| Q3 – Calprotectin >250 | Lab signal | Captures active flares even when diagnosis is coded vaguely; important for disease-activity monitoring arm of QI initiative |

Take the **union of distinct patient IDs** across all three queries for your complete biologic adherence + monitoring cohort. Patients appearing in all three are your highest-confidence, actively-monitored Crohn's cases.

## FHIR Queries for Crohn's Disease Patients with IBD Medications

> ⚠️ **Note on "≥2 Occurrences":** Standard FHIR does not support aggregate count filtering (`COUNT >= 2 GROUP BY patient`). Queries 1 and 2 are structured to return all matching records; patient-level de-duplication and occurrence counting must be performed at the application layer by grouping `Condition.subject` references.

---

### Query 1 — Patient-Centric Cross-Reference (Confirmed Crohn's + IBD Medications)
Finds patients satisfying **both** criteria simultaneously using `_has`:

```
Patient?_has:Condition:patient:code=http://snomed.info/sct|34000006,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K50.00,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K50.10,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K50.80,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K50.90&_has:Condition:patient:verification-status=confirmed&_has:MedicationRequest:patient:code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|41493,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|9524,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1256,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|5026,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|7426,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|185747,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|327361,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|602525,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|358255&_revinclude=Condition:patient&_revinclude=MedicationRequest:patient
```

---

### Query 2 — Confirmed Crohn's Disease Conditions
Retrieves all confirmed Crohn's records; group by `Condition.subject` to find patients with **≥2 entries**:

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|34000006,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K50.00,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K50.10,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K50.80,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K50.90&verification-status=confirmed&_include=Condition:subject
```

---

### Query 3 — IBD Medication Prescriptions

### Query Architecture Summary

| Layer | Approach | Codes Used | Count |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Dx — Crohn's (all subtypes)** | VSAC MITRE value set .348 (39 SNOMED + 28 ICD-10-CM) + ICD-9-CM legacy observed on server | 71 codes total | 2,080 Condition records |

| **IBD Medications** | Verified RxNorm ingredient (IN) codes: mesalamine, sulfasalazine, infliximab, adalimumab, azathioprine, methotrexate, budesonide | 7 codes | 2,328 MedRequest records |

**Runner instructions:**
1. **Query 1** → group by `Condition.subject` → keep patients with **≥ 2** matching records → this is the "documented Crohn's ×2" set
2. **Query 2** → collect distinct patients → this is the "IBD-medicated" set

3-naive.raw_response:
Both queries validated ✅ — 1,559 patients (cross-resource AND) and 2,080 confirmed Crohn's condition instances. All evidence gathered:

---

**Playbooks applied:** 8 (multi-system codes) + 11 (cross-resource AND via `_has`) + 12 (validated case: confirmed dx + IBD medication + ≥2 occurrences)

**Code provenance:**
- **Condition codes** (SNOMED + ICD-10-CM): MITRE VSAC value sets `2.16.840.1.113762.1.4.1032.346` & `347` (active), confirmed on server
- **IBD medications** (RxNorm, all confirmed on server): adalimumab `327361` (530 Rx), infliximab `191831` (555 Rx), azathioprine `1256` (545 Rx), ustekinumab `847083` (414 Rx)
- **verificationStatus=confirmed** applied on both the `_has` chain and the Condition occurrences query
- **≥2 occurrences**: FHIR R4 has no native instance-count filter; Query 2 (`Condition?...`) returns all confirmed Crohn's condition records — the harness/application layer must group by `Condition.subject`, keep patients with count ≥ 2, then intersect with the patient set from Query 1.

3-naive.prompt_text: Find patients with a confirmed diagnosis of Crohn's disease who have been prescribed medications for inflammatory bowel disease treatment and have at least two documented occurrences of the condition

3-broad.raw_response:
`_has` is confirmed working as a true AND (fake code → 0, real codes → 151). 624 patients with general SNOMED Crohn's code; AND with mesalamine drops to 151. Now I have everything needed to compose the final cross-resource AND query with the full validated code set.

**Summary of evidence collected:**
| Element | Codes confirmed on server |
|---|---|
| Crohn's dx (SNOMED CT) | 34000006, 7620006, 56689002, 71833008, 196977009, 38106008, 50440006, 3815005, 1197732001, 1197733006, 397172008, 397173003, 426549001, 414153008, 414154002, 91390005, 56287005 |
| Crohn's dx (ICD-10-CM) | K50.00–K50.919 (full VSAC set) |
| IBD meds (RxNorm) | 52582 (mesalamine), 327361 (adalimumab), 191831 (infliximab), 1256 (azathioprine), 847083 (ustekinumab) |
| `_has` AND support | ✅ confirmed (Azure FHIR R4) |
| verificationStatus=confirmed | Supported |

**Design decisions:**
- **Playbook 11** (`_has` AND): enforces BOTH Crohn's dx AND IBD med on the same patient
- **Confirmed dx**: `_has:Condition:patient:verification-status=confirmed` added as a third constraint
- **≥2 occurrences**: FHIR R4 `_has` has no built-in count modifier; addressed with a second standalone `Condition` query — the harness treats it as the occurrence-evidence set; calling application groups by `.subject` and retains patients with record count ≥ 2, then intersects with the patient set from Query 1

3-expert.raw_response:
All evidence gathered. Here's the complete picture:

---

**Playbooks**: **11 (Cross-resource AND)** + **12 (Validated case = AND of strict criteria)**

**Evidence summary**:
| Signal | Resource | Codes | Server count |
|---|---|---|---|
| Crohn's dx | Condition | SNOMED: 34000006 + 12 subtypes; ICD-10: K50.00–K50.919 (28 codes); ICD-9: 555.x | 2,080 records |
| IBD meds | MedicationRequest | adalimumab 327361; infliximab 191831; azathioprine 1256; ustekinumab 847083 | 530–555 each |
| Dx ∩ Meds | Patient `_has` | Full cross-resource AND | 1,559 patients |

**Design decisions**:
- Verified all 4 medication RxNorm codes directly from server samples — not guessed
- `verificationStatus=confirmed` test returned identical count (all Conditions are confirmed by default on this server)
- **"≥2 occurrences"** is not a native FHIR filter; Query 2 emits all Crohn's Condition records so the harness can group-by-patient and retain those with count ≥ 2
- Full ICD-10-CM complication hierarchy (K50.x11–x19) included to capture all severity variants
- ICD-9-CM codes (555.0/1/2/9) included because they co-exist in records alongside ICD-10 and SNOMED

---

```

Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K50.00&_include=Condition:subject

```

### Explanation:
| Query | Description |
|---|---|
| `SNOMED CT \| 34000006` | Crohn's disease (primary SNOMED concept) |
| `ICD-10-CM \| K50.90` | Crohn's disease, unspecified, without complications |
| `ICD-10-CM \| K50.00` | Crohn's disease of small intestine, without complications |
| `ICD-10-CM \| K50.10` | Crohn's disease of large intestine, without complications |
| `ICD-10-CM \| K50.80` | Other Crohn's disease, without complications |

- **`_include=Condition:subject`** pulls the associated `Patient` resources in the same bundle response.
- Consider also adding **`?clinical-status=active`** if you only want active diagnoses.

|---|---|

1-broad.raw_response:
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|34000006&_include=Condition:subject:Patient
```

---

### 🔍 Query Breakdown

| Component | Value | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| **Resource** | `Condition` | Base resource being searched |
| **`code`** | `http://snomed.info/sct\|34000006` | SNOMED CT code for **Crohn's disease** |
| **`_include`** | `Condition:subject:Patient` | Also return the `Patient` referenced by `Condition.subject` |

---

### 📦 Resulting Bundle Contents

The returned `Bundle` will contain **two entry types**:

1. **`Condition`** entries — matching Crohn's disease conditions
   - `entry.search.mode = "match"`

2. **`Patient`** entries — the patients referenced by those conditions
   - `entry.search.mode = "include"`

---

### 💡 Variations to Consider

**Using ICD-10-CM instead of / in addition to SNOMED:**
```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K50.90&_include=Condition:subject:Patient
```

**Broader Crohn's disease ICD-10 category (all K50.x subtypes):**
```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K50&_include=Condition:subject:Patient
```

**Active conditions only:**
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|34000006&clinical-status=active&_include=Condition:subject:Patient
```

> **Note:** The `:Patient` type qualifier in `_include=Condition:subject:Patient` is a best practice — it explicitly scopes the include to `Patient` resources only, avoiding accidental inclusion of `Group` resources that could also appear in `Condition.subject`.

## How I built this query

### Discovery summary

| Layer | Finding |
|---|---|
| **Server** | FHIR R4 (4.0.1), Azure FHIR Server — no IGs loaded, no US Core profile requirement |
| **Code systems on server** | All three active simultaneously: **SNOMED CT + ICD-10-CM + ICD-9-CM** (confirmed by sampling 3 real Condition records) |
| **VSAC** | Three active MITRE value sets (2025-01-07) — 39 SNOMED codes + 28 ICD-10-CM codes |
| **ICD-9-CM** | Live server samples carry `555.1`; full family = `555.0`, `555.1`, `555.2`, `555.9` |
| **Count validation** | Stable at **2,080 Condition resources** across all code layers |
| **_include validation** | `_count=5` returned 10 entries → 5 Conditions + 5 Patients ✅ |

---

### Why all three code systems matter

Real EHR records on this server carry **all three codings on the same Condition** (e.g., `SNOMED 7620006` + `ICD-10 K50.10` + `ICD-9 555.1`). Any single-system query would still match those records, but if a legacy record was coded only in ICD-9, or a modern record only in SNOMED, a single-system query would miss it. Using all three simultaneously with `OR` semantics (`code=A,B,C`) is both safe and exhaustive.

---

### Code families used

| System | Codes |
|---|---|
| **SNOMED CT** (39 codes) | `34000006` (Crohn's disease — parent), + all anatomic variants: ileum (`38106008`), jejunum (`91390005`), duodenum (`56287005`), colon (`50440006`), large bowel (`7620006`), rectum (`3815005`), small intestine (`56689002`), small+large (`71833008`), terminal ileum (`196977009`), esophagus (`235607002`), stomach (`235664007`), pylorus (`61424003`), pyloric antrum (`70622003`), anal canal (`721702009`), appendix (`733157003`), upper GI (`737195007`), anastomosis (`722850002`), oral soft tissue (`196578009`), gingivae (`234999001`), GI Crohn's (`397172008`), intestine (`397173003`), remission variants (`426549001`, `515623061000119105`, `527788091000119106`, `253855721000119107`, `206138281000119100`), exacerbation (`414153008`, `414154002`), abscess variants, arthritis/iritis co-occurring |
| **ICD-10-CM** (28 codes) | Full `K50.*` family: `K50.00`→`K50.019` (small intestine), `K50.10`→`K50.119` (large intestine), `K50.80`→`K50.819` (both), `K50.90`→`K50.919` (unspecified) — all complication sub-codes |
| **ICD-9-CM** (4 codes) | `555.0` (small), `555.1` (large), `555.2` (small+large), `555.9` (unspecified) |

### `_include` mechanics

`_include=Condition:subject` tells the server: *"for every Condition in the result set, follow the `.subject` reference and pull back the target Patient resource."* The bundle will contain entries of two `resourceType` values: `Condition` (primary match, `search.mode = match`) and `Patient` (pulled in, `search.mode = include`). No second round-trip is needed.

### Key design decisions explained

| Decision | Rationale |
|---|---|
| `_include=Condition:subject` | Tells the server to follow `Condition.subject` → `Patient` and embed those resources in the same Bundle — one HTTP round-trip, no client-side joins |
| All three coding systems in one `code=` param | The server sample showed live records with SNOMED, ICD-10-CM **and** ICD-9-CM; a single comma-separated list is a logical OR, capturing historic (ICD-9) and current (ICD-10/SNOMED) records |
| 39 SNOMED subtypes (not just umbrella `34000006`) | The umbrella code won't match records coded to a specific site (e.g. `196977009` terminal ileum); including all subtypes prevents undercounting |
| All 28 K50.x leaf codes | ICD-10-CM requires the full 5-character complication code; K50.9 without the sixth digit may not exist as a valid billable code on this server |

1-naive.raw_response:
Fecal calprotectin is the most common clinical measurement for intestinal inflammation. The queries below use the standard LOINC code and filter for high/critically-high interpretation flags, including the associated Patient resources.

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|55798-0&interpretation=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/v3-ObservationInterpretation|H&_include=Observation:patient
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|55798-0&interpretation=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/v3-ObservationInterpretation|HH&_include=Observation:patient
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|35549-8&interpretation=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/v3-ObservationInterpretation|H&_include=Observation:patient
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|35549-8&interpretation=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/v3-ObservationInterpretation|HH&_include=Observation:patient
```

**Notes:**
| LOINC Code | Description |
|---|---|
| `55798-0` | Calprotectin \[Mass/mass\] in Stool (μg/g) — most common |
| `35549-8` | Calprotectin \[Mass/volume\] in Stool (μg/mL) |

- **`H`** = High (typically **> 50 μg/g**; clinically significant often **> 200 μg/g**)
- **`HH`** = Critically High

If you'd prefer a **value-based threshold** (e.g., > 200 μg/g), use:
```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|55798-0&value-quantity=gt200|http://unitsofmeasure.org|ug/g&_include=Observation:patient
```
1-naive.prompt_text: Find patients with high calprotectin.

1-broad.raw_response:
## FHIR Query: Elevated Fecal Calprotectin (> 250 µg/g)

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|58454-2&value-quantity=gt250||ug/g&_include=Observation:patient
```

---

### Query Breakdown

| Component | Value | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **Resource** | `Observation` | Lab/diagnostic result |
| **LOINC Code** | `58454-2` | *Calprotectin [Mass/mass] in Stool* |
| **Threshold** | `gt250` | Values **> 250 µg/g** |
| **UCUM Unit** | `ug/g` | Micrograms per gram of stool |
| **`_include`** | `Observation:patient` | Pulls linked `Patient` resources |

---

### Clinical Context

| Range | Interpretation |
|---|---|
| **< 50 µg/g** | Normal — IBD unlikely |
| **50–250 µg/g** | Borderline — repeat or investigate |
| **> 250 µg/g** *(this query)* | **Elevated — active IBD suggested** |
| **> 500 µg/g** | Highly elevated — likely active IBD flare |

> ⚕️ **Note:** Fecal calprotectin (`58454-2`) distinguishes **organic** intestinal inflammation (Crohn's, UC) from **functional** disorders (IBS). Results should be correlated with clinical symptoms and endoscopic/imaging findings.
1-broad.prompt_text: Find patients with elevated fecal calprotectin (a stool marker of intestinal inflammation) — values greater than 250 mcg/g of stool, suggesting active IBD.

1-expert.raw_response:
## FHIR Query: Elevated Fecal Calprotectin

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|38445-3&value-quantity=gt250|http://unitsofmeasure.org|ug/g
```

---

### Query Breakdown

| Component | Value | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **Resource** | `Observation` | Lab/clinical observation |
| **LOINC Code** | `38445-3` | Calprotectin [Mass/mass] in Stool |
| **Comparator** | `gt250` | Greater than 250 |
| **Unit System** | `http://unitsofmeasure.org` | UCUM (Unified Code for Units of Measure) |
| **Unit Code** | `ug/g` | Micrograms per gram (UCUM for mcg/g) |

---

### Key Notes

- ✅ **`ug/g`** is the correct **UCUM code** for micrograms per gram (`mcg/g` is not standard UCUM)
- ✅ Including **`http://unitsofmeasure.org`** as the unit system ensures precise unit matching and avoids ambiguity
- ⚠️ Your original query (`||ug/g`) omits the unit system — this may work on some servers but is less reliable

```

### Query Breakdown

| RxNorm Code | Drug | Class | Cross-Indications |
|---|---|---|---|
| `52582` | Mesalamine (5-ASA) | Aminosalicylate | UC only (Crohn's-specific here) |
| `191831` | Infliximab | Anti-TNF biologic | RA, AS, psoriasis, UC |
| `327361` | Adalimumab | Anti-TNF biologic | RA, AS, psoriasis, UC |
| `847083` | Ustekinumab | IL-12/23 inhibitor | Plaque psoriasis, PsA, UC |
| `1256` | Azathioprine | Immunomodulator | Lupus, organ transplant, IBD |

### Important Caveats

1. **Cross-indication noise**: This query returns **all** MedicationRequests for these drugs regardless of indication. To narrow to Crohn's disease specifically, consider a **chained or patient-level filter** against a Crohn's `Condition` (ICD-10 `K50.*` / SNOMED `34000006`), e.g.:
   ```
   MedicationRequest?code=...&_has:Condition:patient:code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K50
   ```

2. **Medication as Reference**: If your server stores medications as `medicationReference` (pointing to a `Medication` resource) rather than `medicationCodeableConcept`, the `code` parameter may not match. You may need:
   ```
   MedicationRequest?medication.code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|52582,...
   ```
